name: research
description: v2.7.2 - Agent-driven R&D protocol with typed hypothesis generation, plan-visual review, prior-work grounding, result analysis, and paper-grade reports.

interface.shortDescription: Agent-driven R&D protocol with plan review and explanation-centered result analysis
interface.longDescription: v2.7.2 - A protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three OECD Frascati Manual 2015 categories: basic_research, applied_research, and experimental_development. Includes the research lifecycle (research situation diagnosis → hypothesis type selection → hypothesis-generation record → prior-work grounding → plan → plan review → execution → result analysis → claim → decision). Keeps the timing boundary explicit: Plan and Plan review record pre-result commitments such as question, hypothesis type, prediction or expected observation, primary measure, fair comparator or control when required, evidence route, artifacts, and stop / branch criteria; Result analysis records post-result explanations only after evidence exists. Requires typed hypothesis generation before planning from research ideas: diagnose available and missing material, choose predictive / performance, mechanistic, causal / intervention, descriptive / characterization, theoretical, or mixed with a declared primary type, and produce the selected hypothesis-generation record. Mechanism hypothesis records are required only when the selected claim asks why/how through entities, activities, process, organization, or mechanism of action. Predictive / performance records require a fair comparator or baseline and support / rejection thresholds without forcing why-it-worked decomposition. Requires Plan sections to start with a Plan visual when architecture, data/evaluation flow, mechanism, system boundary, variable space, decision flow, or derivation structure should be inspectable. Requires plan-scoped literature survey evidence before the Plan section, a Citation-use map for how each cited work is used, verifiable retrieval-unavailable evidence with claim-scope narrowing, and mid-execution literature updates when new methods, comparators, contradictions, or missing-baseline signals appear. Adds research-plan-review for plan-path-only review before execution and keeps research-result-analysis as the plan-path-only result-analysis skill after execution. Plan review acts as a stop gate for wrong, unsupported, or unverified premises and for hypothesis validation methods that cannot distinguish the hypothesis from plausible alternatives, while preserving narrowed observation, measurement-construction, and exploratory plans. Result analysis reconstructs evidence independently and explains why the result happened through candidate explanations, evidence for/against, procedure/artifact explanations, live alternatives, and discriminating next analyses. Methods reproducibility is enforced; experiment-level replicability is the agent's discretion, while provenance and claim-to-artifact checks are treated as audit/evidence-integrity controls. Reports remain paper-grade with required Theory/Related Work/Ablation/Discussion/References sections and statistical reporting minimums for numeric evidence.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
